summary.intent: Verify completeness of a layered tool policy implementation (discussion #641) and fix any missing pieces like config template documentation.
summary.outcome: Identified three gaps (sender_id in channel_binding_from_runtime_context, missing config template examples, missing docs), determined one was pre-existing/out-of-scope, and updated config template with commented examples for per-provider and per-channel tool policy fields.

repo: CLAUDE.md requires updating config template and docs when adding user-facing config fields
Policy resolution has 6 layers: global, per-provider, per-agent-preset, per-channel-group, per-sender
sender_id flows through params["channel"]["sender_id"] (serialized ChannelMessageMeta) for policy resolution, not through channel_binding_from_runtime_context
channel_binding_from_runtime_context is used for hook context, not policy — a separate code path from build_policy_context

code.finding: sender_id hardcoded to None in channel_binding_from_runtime_context — pre-existing gap affecting hooks (related to discussion #640), not introduced by this change
Config template updated with commented examples for per-provider policy and per-channel group/sender tool policy overrides

workflow: After implementing a feature, grep for struct fields initialized to None/default to catch unpopulated fields
Distinguish between pre-existing gaps vs. regressions introduced — pre-existing issues can be noted without blocking the current PR

friction: sender_id gap in channel_binding_from_runtime_context was initially flagged as missing but required tracing two separate code paths to confirm it was pre-existing and out of scope
open_items: sender_id not populated in channel_binding_from_runtime_context for hook context — tracked as related to discussion #640
No dedicated tool-policy documentation page exists; existing docs only cover agent preset scope — a full page would be valuable but was deferred as out of scope for this PR
